question: Is the yellow rubber duck visible in the generated image?
Is the rainbow swim ring visible in the generated image?
Is the rubber duck positioned in the left lane area of the pool?
Is the swim ring positioned on the right side of the pool, near the lounge chairs?
Does the rubber duck retain the visual identity of the reference image (yellow body, orange beak, black eyes)?
Does the swim ring retain the visual identity of the reference image (rainbow gradient, toroidal shape)?
Are the relative sizes of the objects realistic (duck significantly smaller than the swim ring and appropriate for the pool scale)?
Do both objects display a realistic waterline indicating partial submersion, rather than hovering above the surface?
Are visible water ripples or surface deformations present around the base of the objects where they meet the water?
Are reflections of the duck and swim ring visible on the water surface?
Do the object reflections blend naturally with the existing ceiling light reflections on the water surface?
Does the lighting on the inserted objects match the soft, diffuse ambient lighting of the indoor pool scene?
Are the background elements (floor-to-ceiling windows, outdoor vegetation, ceiling lights) preserved without distortion?
Are the pool structure and lane markings preserved and visible (except where obscured by the inserted objects)?
Is the image free from obvious blending artifacts (e.g., jagged edges, white halos, or unnatural cutouts) around the inserted objects?
